Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given 15 events for Machine M1 over a short time period The goal is to group these events into phases activities based on the sensor readings

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Events 1 2 3 13 14 15 Temperature around 20 21 C Pressure 1 bar Vibration 0 Hz Material Flow 0 units s Tool Position 0 mm Energy 0 40 to 0 50 kWh These events look like the machine is idle or at rest

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Events 4 5 6 Temperature rises from 50 to 58 C Pressure 1 bar initially then 1 5 bar Vibration 30 to 38 Hz Material Flow 5 units s Tool Position moves from 5 to 10 mm Energy 2 00 to 3 00 kWh This pattern suggests a cutting operation The rising temperature and vibration along with material flow indicate that the machine is actively cutting metal

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Events 7 8 Temperature 40 C Pressure 2 bar Vibration 10 Hz Material Flow 2 units s Tool Position 15 mm Energy 2 00 kWh This might be a phase of assembling parts The tool is at a fixed position and there is some vibration and material flow but less than during cutting

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Event 9 Temperature 80 C Pressure 2 bar Vibration 50 Hz Material Flow 0 units s Tool Position 15 mm Energy 5 00 kWh This is a high energy event with high temperature and vibration This might be welding

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Events 10 11 12 Temperature drops from 30 to 25 C Pressure 1 bar Vibration 1 to 5 Hz Material Flow 0 to 1 unit s Tool Position moves to 20 mm and stays Energy 1 00 to 1 60 kWh This phase might be quality inspection The tool is at a new position 20 mm and the machine is running at a lower energy level which could be for scanning or moving the product for inspection

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore we can group the events as follows Events 1 2 3 13 14 15 Machine Idle or Initial Setup Events 4 5 6 Cutting Metal Events 7 8 Assembling Parts Event 9 Welding Events 10 11 12 Quality Inspection

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But note after event 9 welding we have events 10 11 12 quality inspection and then events 13 14 15 idle This sequence makes sense the machine goes through a complete cycle

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the entire cycle for one product or batch is 1 Idle events 1 3 2 Cutting 4 6 3 Assembling 7 8 4 Welding 9 5 Quality Inspection 10 12 6 Idle again 13 15

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However the time stamps are sequential and the events are for one machine in a short time from 08 00 00 to 08 01 10 It is more likely one cycle

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We must assign labels to each event Since multiple events in a phase share the same label we can assign Events 1 2 3 Initial Setup or Idle Events 4 5 6 Cutting Metal Events 7 8 Assembling Parts Event 9 Welding Events 10 11 12 Quality Inspection Events 13 14 15 Idle

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Explanation for each group 1 Initial Setup Idle Events 1 3 13 15 These events show the machine at rest with no material flow no vibration and low energy The tool is at position 0 This is when the machine is either starting up or returning to a rest state after a cycle 2 Cutting Metal Events 4 6 There is a sudden rise in temperature from 20 C to 58 C vibration from 0 to 38 Hz and material flow 5 units s The tool moves from 5 mm to 10 mm Energy consumption is high 2 00 to 3 00 kWh These are typical signs of a cutting operation 3 Assembling Parts Events 7 8 The temperature remains around 40 C pressure increases to 2 bar vibration is moderate 10 Hz and material flow is reduced 2 units s The tool position is fixed at 15 mm Energy is still high 2 00 kWh This phase might involve placing and securing parts 4 Welding Event 9 A spike in temperature 80 C and vibration 50 Hz and the highest energy consumption 5 00 kWh occurs Material flow stops 0 units s and the tool remains fixed at 15 mm This is characteristic of a welding operation 5 Quality Inspection Events 10 12 After welding the machine cools down temperature drops to 30 C then 25 C vibration is low 1 5 Hz and material flow is minimal 0 to 1 unit s The tool moves to 20 mm and stays Energy is moderate 1 00 to 1 60 kWh This suggests the product is being inspected
